title: Dombeya populnea
intro: Dombeya populnea, also known as the Populnea tree or Dombeya, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its showy, fragrant flowers and deciduous foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dombeya populnea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dombeya populnea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in aromatherapy to benefit from its fragrance and potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Dombeya populnea is classified within the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family consists of approximately 200-250 species, including various types of hibiscus, okra, and cotton. The Dombeya populnea is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Indian Ocean region and is often found in coastal areas and islands. Its classification as a member of the Malvaceae family suggests its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. Within the Malvaceae family, Dombeya populnea is part of the genus Dombeya, which comprises approximately 60-70 species.
uses: Dombeya populnea has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
